  [--region ]
Parameters Description
  • --cname-prefix (string): [Required] Desired CNAME prefix (subdomain)
Return Value
  • Available (Boolean): True if available
  • FullyQualifiedCNAME (String): Full domain if available
Example 1: Check Availability
#!/bin/bash
# Check if anihpj-prod is available
aws elasticbeanstalk check-dns-availability \
  --cname-prefix anihpj-prod
{ "Available": true, "FullyQualifiedCNAME": "anihpj-prod.us-east-1.elasticbeanstalk.com" }
# DNS available from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost print(f"anihpj-prod.elasticbeanstalk.com available!")
Example 2: Check Unavailable Name
#!/bin/bash
# Check already taken name
aws elasticbeanstalk check-dns-availability \
  --cname-prefix production
{ "Available": false }
# DNS taken from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost print(f"Name already in use")
Example 3: Find Available Variation
#!/bin/bash
# Try variations until available
for suffix in "" "-app" "-web" "-api"; do
  NAME="anihpj-jobpost${suffix}"
  RESULT=$(aws elasticbeanstalk check-dns-availability \
    --cname-prefix $NAME \
    --query 'Available' --output text)
  if [ "$RESULT" = "True" ]; then
    echo "✓ Available: $NAME"
    break
  fi
done
✓ Available: anihpj-jobpost-app
# Find available from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost print(f"Found available name")
Example 4: Check Multiple Environments
#!/bin/bash
# Check all environment names at once
for env in anihpj-dev anihpj-staging anihpj-prod; do
  STATUS=$(aws elasticbeanstalk check-dns-availability \
    --cname-prefix $env --query 'Available' --output text)
  echo "$env: $STATUS"
done
anihpj-dev: True anihpj-staging: True anihpj-prod: True
# Multi-check from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost print(f"All env names available")
Example 5: Get Full CNAME
#!/bin/bash
# Get complete URL for documentation
CNAME=$(aws elasticbeanstalk check-dns-availability \
  --cname-prefix anihpj-prod \
  --query 'FullyQualifiedCNAME' \
  --output text)

echo "Environment URL will be: http://$CNAME"
Environment URL will be: http://anihpj-prod.us-east-1.elasticbeanstalk.com
# Full URL from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost print(f"Django app URL confirmed")

create-storage-location

Creates an S3 bucket for Elastic Beanstalk to store application versions, logs, and other artifacts. Called automatically when needed, but can be invoked manually.

Syntax
aws elasticbeanstalk create-storage-location \
  [--region ]
Parameters Description
  • --region (string): AWS region for the bucket
Return Value
  • S3Bucket (String): Name of the created bucket
Example 1: Create Storage Bucket
#!/bin/bash
# Create Elastic Beanstalk storage
BUCKET=$(aws elasticbeanstalk create-storage-location \
  --query 'S3Bucket' \
  --output text)

echo "Storage bucket: $BUCKET"
{ "S3Bucket": "elasticbeanstalk-us-east-1-123456789012" }
# Create bucket from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost print(f"S3 storage bucket created")
Example 2: Create in Specific Region
#!/bin/bash
# Create storage in eu-west-1
aws elasticbeanstalk create-storage-location \
  --region eu-west-1
{ "S3Bucket": "elasticbeanstalk-eu-west-1-123456789012" }
# EU bucket from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost print(f"EU storage created")
Example 3: Get or Create Storage
#!/bin/bash
# Ensure storage exists
BUCKET=$(aws elasticbeanstalk create-storage-location \
  --query 'S3Bucket' --output text)

echo "Using bucket: $BUCKET"

# List current contents
aws s3 ls s3://$BUCKET/
Using bucket: elasticbeanstalk-us-east-1-123456789012 PRE resources/
# Get storage from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost print(f"Storage bucket ready")
Example 4: Upload App Version
#!/bin/bash
# Get bucket and upload Django app
BUCKET=$(aws elasticbeanstalk create-storage-location \
  --query 'S3Bucket' --output text)

# Upload application bundle
aws s3 cp anihpj-django.zip s3://$BUCKET/anihpj/versions/

echo "✓ App uploaded to EB storage"
✓ App uploaded to EB storage
# Upload app from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost print(f"Django bundle uploaded")
Example 5: Export Bucket Name
#!/bin/bash
# Save bucket name for CI/CD
aws elasticbeanstalk create-storage-location \
  --query 'S3Bucket' --output text > .eb-bucket

echo "EB_BUCKET=$(cat .eb-bucket)" >> $GITHUB_ENV

echo "✓ Bucket name exported for deployment"
✓ Bucket name exported for deployment
# Export bucket from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost print(f"Bucket name saved for CI")

request-environment-info

Initiates compilation of environment logs or bundle info. The request is asynchronous - use retrieve-environment-info to get results.

Syntax
aws elasticbeanstalk request-environment-info \
  --info-type  \
  [--environment-id ] \
  [--environment-name ] \
  [--region ]
Parameters Description
  • --info-type (string): [Required] Type: tail | bundle
  • --environment-name (string): Environment name
  • --environment-id (string): Environment ID
Return Value
  • None: Request initiated asynchronously
Example 1: Request Tail Logs
#!/bin/bash
# Request last 100 lines of logs
aws elasticbeanstalk request-environment-info \
  --environment-name anihpj-prod \
  --info-type tail

echo "✓ Log request initiated - retrieve in ~30 seconds"
✓ Log request initiated - retrieve in ~30 seconds
# Request logs from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost print(f"Tail logs requested")
Example 2: Request Full Bundle
#!/bin/bash
# Request full log bundle (all logs zipped)
aws elasticbeanstalk request-environment-info \
  --environment-name anihpj-prod \
  --info-type bundle

echo "✓ Full bundle requested - may take 1-2 minutes"
✓ Full bundle requested - may take 1-2 minutes
# Request bundle from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost print(f"Full bundle requested")
Example 3: Request by Environment ID
#!/bin/bash
# Request using environment ID
ENV_ID=$(aws elasticbeanstalk describe-environments \
  --environment-names anihpj-prod \
  --query 'Environments[0].EnvironmentId' --output text)

aws elasticbeanstalk request-environment-info \
  --environment-id $ENV_ID \
  --info-type tail
✓ Request submitted for e-abc123
# By ID from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost print(f"Request by env ID")
Example 4: Request and Wait
#!/bin/bash
# Request logs and wait for availability
aws elasticbeanstalk request-environment-info \
  --environment-name anihpj-prod \
  --info-type tail

echo "Waiting for logs..."
sleep 30

aws elasticbeanstalk retrieve-environment-info \
  --environment-name anihpj-prod \
  --info-type tail
Waiting for logs... {EnvironmentInfo returned}
# Request + wait from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost print(f"Logs retrieved after wait")
Example 5: Debug Workflow
#!/bin/bash
# Complete debug workflow
echo "Requesting Django error logs..."
aws elasticbeanstalk request-environment-info \
  --environment-name anihpj-prod \
  --info-type tail

echo "Request submitted - use retrieve-environment-info to get results"
Requesting Django error logs... Request submitted - use retrieve-environment-info to get results
# Debug flow from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost print(f"Debug workflow started")

retrieve-environment-info

Retrieves environment logs or bundle previously requested with request-environment-info. Returns S3 URLs to download the information.

Syntax
aws elasticbeanstalk retrieve-environment-info \
  --info-type  \
  [--environment-id ] \
  [--environment-name ] \
  [--region ]
Parameters Description
  • --info-type (string): [Required] Type: tail | bundle
  • --environment-name (string): Environment name
  • --environment-id (string): Environment ID
Return Value
  • EnvironmentInfo (Array): Info with S3 URLs and timestamps
Example 1: Retrieve Tail Logs
#!/bin/bash
# Get tail logs after request
aws elasticbeanstalk retrieve-environment-info \
  --environment-name anihpj-prod \
  --info-type tail
{ "EnvironmentInfo": [ { "InfoType": "tail", "Ec2InstanceId": "i-0abc123def456", "SampleTimestamp": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z", "Message": "https://elasticbeanstalk-us-east-1-123456789012.s3.amazonaws.com/..." } ] }
# Get logs from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost print(f"Log URLs returned")
Example 2: Download Logs
#!/bin/bash
# Download log files
URLS=$(aws elasticbeanstalk retrieve-environment-info \
  --environment-name anihpj-prod \
  --info-type tail \
  --query 'EnvironmentInfo[].Message' \
  --output text)

for url in $URLS; do
  curl -s "$url" >> django-logs.txt
done

echo "✓ Logs downloaded to django-logs.txt"
✓ Logs downloaded to django-logs.txt
# Download logs from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost print(f"Django logs downloaded")
Example 3: Get Bundle URL
#!/bin/bash
# Get full bundle download URL
BUNDLE_URL=$(aws elasticbeanstalk retrieve-environment-info \
  --environment-name anihpj-prod \
  --info-type bundle \
  --query 'EnvironmentInfo[0].Message' \
  --output text)

wget -O full-logs.zip "$BUNDLE_URL"
echo "✓ Bundle downloaded"
✓ Bundle downloaded
# Bundle download from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost print(f"Full bundle downloaded")
Example 4: Get Logs Per Instance
#!/bin/bash
# Get logs organized by instance
aws elasticbeanstalk retrieve-environment-info \
  --environment-name anihpj-prod \
  --info-type tail \
  --query 'EnvironmentInfo[].{Instance:Ec2InstanceId,URL:Message}' \
  --output table
----------------------------------------------------- | EnvironmentInfo | +-----------------+----------------------------------+ | Instance | URL | +-----------------+----------------------------------+ | i-0abc123 | https://s3.amazonaws.com/... | | i-0def456 | https://s3.amazonaws.com/... | +-----------------+----------------------------------+
# Per instance from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost print(f"Logs per instance")
Example 5: Complete Log Retrieval
#!/bin/bash
# Full workflow: request + retrieve
aws elasticbeanstalk request-environment-info \
  --environment-name anihpj-prod --info-type tail
sleep 30

aws elasticbeanstalk retrieve-environment-info \
  --environment-name anihpj-prod \
  --info-type tail \
  --query 'EnvironmentInfo[].Message' \
  --output text | xargs -I{} curl -s "{}" > all-logs.txt

echo "✓ All logs compiled"
✓ All logs compiled
# Full workflow from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost print(f"Complete log retrieval done")

💾 Storage Services

AWS Storage services provide scalable and durable storage for your Django application data. This section covers S3 for object storage, EFS for shared file systems, and EBS for block storage volumes. Learn how to store static files, media uploads, and database backups for your jobpost application.

Amazon S3

S3 section will be added in STEP 6 - Coming soon...

Amazon EFS

EFS section will be added in STEP 7 - Coming soon...

Amazon EBS

EBS section will be added in STEP 8 - Coming soon...

🛠️ Developer Tools

AWS Developer Tools provide comprehensive CI/CD capabilities for automating your Django application deployment pipeline. This section covers CodePipeline for orchestration, CodeBuild for compilation, CodeDeploy for deployment, and CodeCommit for source control.

AWS CodePipeline

CodePipeline section will be added in STEP 9 - Coming soon...

AWS CodeBuild

CodeBuild section will be added in STEP 10 - Coming soon...

AWS CodeDeploy

CodeDeploy section will be added in STEP 11 - Coming soon...

AWS CodeCommit

CodeCommit section will be added in STEP 12 - Coming soon...

⚙️ Management & Governance

AWS Management services help you monitor, manage, and govern your infrastructure. This section covers CloudWatch for monitoring, CloudFormation for infrastructure as code, Systems Manager for operational management, and CloudTrail for auditing.

AWS CloudWatch

CloudWatch section will be added in STEP 13 - Coming soon...

🐳 Containers

AWS Container services provide fully managed container orchestration and registry. This section covers ECS for container orchestration, EKS for Kubernetes, and ECR for container registry. Learn how to deploy your anihpj Django application in containers.

Amazon ECS

ECS section will be added in STEP 17 - Coming soon...

Amazon EKS

🔒 Security & Identity

AWS Security services help you secure your applications and data. This section covers IAM for identity and access management, Cognito for user authentication, Certificate Manager for SSL/TLS, Secrets Manager for credential management, and KMS for encryption key management.

AWS IAM

🔗 Application Integration

AWS Application Integration services enable loosely coupled, distributed architectures. This section covers SQS for message queuing, SNS for pub/sub messaging, API Gateway for REST APIs, and Step Functions for serverless workflows.

Amazon SQS

📊 Analytics & Monitoring

AWS Analytics services help you gain insights from your data. This section covers CloudWatch Logs Insights for log analysis, X-Ray for application tracing, and Athena for SQL queries on S3 data.

CloudWatch Logs Insights

💾 Backup & Disaster Recovery

AWS Backup services protect your data against loss. This section covers AWS Backup for centralized backup management and S3 Glacier for long-term archival storage.

AWS Backup

pytest integration for testing Django applications and AWS CLI commands. This section covers unit tests, integration tests, and fixtures for testing the jobpost application models with AWS services.
